ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 covers bubble tea generically; no specific code exists for the no-milk, no-topping variant. Closest parent match used and documented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers flavoured tea-based beverages; no dedicated code for bubble tea without milk or topping. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured bubble tea beverage category as of 2026.

main_ingredients: tea base (black tea, green tea, or fruit tea)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is one medium takeaway cup of approximately 500 ml, equivalent to 500 g, as commonly sold at Singaporean bubble tea outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured bubble tea no milk (no topping) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for 'Flavoured bubble tea no milk (no topping)', cr_id D08010026, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages and tea-based drinks
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for flavoured bubble tea without milk or topping. GI of 70 is estimated by analogy to sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages and sweetened tea drinks (GI 65–72) reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) AJCN international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 37.45) / 100 ≈ 26.2, based on a 500 g standard serving containing 37.45 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.49 g/100 g) classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; independent blood pressure elevation risk regardless of very low sodium (1 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage at standard sweetness levels, as sugar-sweetened beverages independently elevate blood pressure via insulin-mediated sympathetic activation (AHA 2018); request 0% sugar or choose unsweetened tea alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 7.49 g sugar/100 g, zero dietary fibre, and high GI (70); liquid sugar absorbed rapidly causing acute postprandial glycaemic spike
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage at standard sweetness, as the high sugar load with zero fibre and high GI (70) produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, request 0% sugar and limit to a small portion (ADA SSB guidance).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at this sugar concentration (7.49 g/100 g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased CVD risk, and weight gain; fat and sodium are negligible
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage due to its sugar-sweetened beverage classification; habitual SSB intake is independently associated with increased CVD mortality risk (AHA/ACC 2019); choose unsweetened tea or water instead.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (7.49 g/100 g) with zero fibre and no anti-inflammatory components promotes pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid sugar-sweetened beverages at this sugar level, as dietary sugar promotes inflammatory cytokine production; choose unsweetened green or herbal teas with anti-inflammatory polyphenols instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Bubble Tea, No Milk, No Topping is a freshly made, fruit or tea-based sweetened cold beverage sold widely at bubble tea outlets across Singapore and Southeast Asia. This variant contains no milk and no customisable toppings such as tapioca pearls, jelly, or pudding, making it lighter in texture and lower in total carbohydrate than the full-topping version. The drink is prepared by combining a tea base — typically black tea, green tea, or a fruit-flavoured infusion — with flavouring syrup and sugar, then shaken or blended with ice. The result is a sweet, refreshing, lightly effervescent beverage with a clean tea or fruit flavour and no creamy mouthfeel. Per 100 g, it provides 29 kcal, 7.49 g carbohydrate (entirely from sugar), 0.01 g protein, 0 g fat, and 1 mg sodium. Despite the absence of toppings, the sugar content of 7.49 g per 100 g classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA, AHA, and HPB definitions. A standard 500 ml serving delivers approximately 37.5 g of free sugar and a glycaemic load of approximately 26, making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The beverage is dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free. It is commonly consumed as a snack or breakfast accompaniment in Singapore's hawker and café culture. Requesting 0% sugar is the primary mitigation strategy for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id D08010026) with a reported confidence of 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. This is an exact match to an approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sugar-sweetened tea-based beverages, documented as such.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — this is a water-based sweetened tea beverage containing no added fat by formulation (confirmed zero). Saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by the same source and formulation basis. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — this beverage contains no fibre-contributing ingredients (confirmed zero). Protein_g is 0.01 g/100 g (trace level from tea solids), not zero. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profile for this freshly made flavoured bubble tea variant is not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry D08010026 or in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent product as of 2026. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened fruit-flavoured beverages and sweetened tea drinks from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) AJCN international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the approved GI database. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (70 × 37.45 g carbohydrate per 500 g serving) / 100 ≈ 26.2. FoodOn and FoodEx2 ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact codes exist for the no-milk, no-topping bubble tea variant.
